description_long: This object is a classic chessboard with a checkered playing surface composed of alternating black and white squares. The board is flat and rectangular, with a slightly raised edge that forms a border around the playing area. The surface shows some subtle wear and discoloration, giving it a slightly aged or used appearance. Two large, blue chess pieces—a rook and a bishop—are positioned on the board, both rendered with faceted, geometric details and a glossy finish. The chessboard itself appears to be made of a solid, dense material, possibly wood or composite, with a printed or painted checkered pattern. The border is plain, with no visible ornamentation or inscriptions, and the sides of the board are a muted reddish-brown color.
description: A rectangular chessboard with alternating black and white squares, bordered by a plain, reddish-brown edge and featuring two large, glossy blue chess pieces.
description_view_0: The chessboard is viewed from an angle showing the rook on the left and the bishop in the center, with the checkered pattern and reddish-brown border clearly visible.
description_view_1: This view is directly aligned with the rook and bishop, emphasizing their geometric shapes and the symmetry of the board's grid.
description_view_2: The board is seen from the opposite side, with the bishop now on the left and the rook on the right, highlighting the alternating square pattern and the clean border.
description_view_3: A direct view along the length of the board, focusing on the bishop and the checkered surface, with the reddish-brown border running along the sides.
attribute_board_pattern: The playing surface features a regular grid of alternating black and white squares, arranged in a classic checkered pattern. Each square is perfectly square-shaped, and the pattern is uninterrupted across the board. The surface shows some subtle discoloration and wear, adding a touch of realism.
attribute_border_design: The border is a plain, slightly raised edge with a reddish-brown color, made of the same material as the board. It is unadorned, with no visible ornamentation, inscriptions, or markings, and has a smooth finish.
